description: Common-sense reference that applies to most modern condensing combi and system boilers. Use this when the exact brand or model isn't known.

faultCodes.title: Boiler locked out (generic)
faultCodes.summary: The boiler has shut itself down to a safe state and needs a manual reset. Most lockouts clear with a single reset — if it locks out again, stop resetting and diagnose.
likelyCauses: Ignition failure — no gas, faulty electrode, or blocked condensate
Overheat or flow-limit trip — poor circulation or sludge
Low pressure cut-out — leak or expansion vessel failure
Fan or flue fault — blocked flue or worn fan bearings
Component fault detected by PCB — sensor, valve, or wiring
quickChecks: Read the exact code displayed — then jump to the brand-specific page for precise diagnosis
Confirm gas supply (check other appliances), electrical supply, and water pressure before pressing reset
Press reset once and observe. If it locks out again within 10 minutes, do not keep resetting — diagnose the root cause
Check the condensate pipe isn't frozen or blocked (very common winter call-out)

faultCodes.title: Ignition fault (generic)
faultCodes.summary: Boiler cannot establish or maintain flame. This is the single most common boiler fault across all brands.
likelyCauses: No gas or low gas pressure — check meter and other appliances
Ignition electrode worn, cracked, or dirty
Flame-sense electrode dirty or glazed — won't detect flame
Condensate trap blocked — backs up and floods the burner
Gas valve not opening — faulty coil or stuck valve
Frozen condensate pipe — extremely common in winter
quickChecks: Confirm gas at other appliances (hob, fire) — if no gas anywhere, contact supplier
Clean and gap the ignition and flame-sense electrode(s)
Drain and clean the condensate trap and siphon
Measure inlet gas pressure at the test point under full load — should be 20 mbar for natural gas
In cold weather, check external condensate pipe for ice blockage — pour warm water to thaw

faultCodes.title: Overheat / limit trip (generic)
faultCodes.summary: Primary flow temperature rose above the safe limit (typically 95–105°C). The boiler has shut down to prevent damage. Usually caused by circulation problems.
likelyCauses: Poor circulation — pump failed, airlocked, or valve closed on the system
Scale or magnetite sludge in heat exchanger restricting flow
Overheat stat or flow NTC faulty — giving false readings
System bypass set too high — not enough water flowing through radiators
quickChecks: Verify pump is running — feel for vibration and warmth on pump body
Check system pressure is at 1.0–1.5 bar cold
Bleed radiators and the auto air vent — trapped air prevents circulation
Check inhibitor level and inspect for sludge — black water when bleeding indicates magnetite
Feel flow and return pipes — large differential (>20°C) suggests poor circulation

faultCodes.title: Low pressure (generic)
faultCodes.summary: System pressure has fallen below the minimum operating level (typically below 0.5 bar). The boiler may not fire until pressure is restored.
likelyCauses: Slow system leak — radiator valve, pipe joint, or auto air vent dripping
Expansion vessel lost charge — diaphragm failed or Schrader valve leaking
PRV weeping — passing water out of the discharge pipe
Recently bled radiators without topping up pressure
quickChecks: Top up to 1.0–1.5 bar via the filling loop (close it afterwards!)
Walk the system checking for damp spots at every joint and valve
Check the PRV discharge pipe outside for dripping
Check expansion vessel pre-charge with a tyre gauge on the Schrader valve (typically 0.75 bar cold)
If pressure drops repeatedly (weekly), a systematic leak search is needed

faultCodes.title: Fan fault (generic)
faultCodes.summary: Fan speed isn't meeting the PCB's demand. The boiler will not attempt ignition if the fan can't prove correct air flow.
likelyCauses: Worn fan bearings — common after 8–10 years
Blocked flue — debris, birds' nest, or ice in winter
Tacho or supply wiring fault — loose connector or damaged cable
PCB fan driver circuit failed
Heavy condensate sitting in the fan housing
quickChecks: Listen to the fan on start-up — grinding or scraping noise means worn bearings
Spin the fan by hand (power off) — it should spin freely with no resistance
Inspect flue terminal outside for blockage or heavy condensate pooling
Check 230V supply to the fan and the tacho signal back to the PCB
Inspect the fan connector — corrosion is common on the tacho pins

faultCodes.title: Sensor fault (generic)
faultCodes.summary: An NTC thermistor or pressure sensor is reading outside its expected range. The boiler may still operate in a limited mode or lock out depending on which sensor has failed.
likelyCauses: Failed NTC sensor — open circuit (reads too high) or short circuit (reads too low)
Unplugged or loose connector — vibration can work connectors free over time
Damaged wiring loom — common after other work near the boiler
Moisture ingress into connector — causes corrosion and erratic readings
quickChecks: Re-seat the sensor connector firmly — it should click into place
Measure NTC resistance with a multimeter — typically ~10kΩ at 25°C (check manufacturer chart)
Inspect wiring for damage, especially where it runs near hot surfaces
Check connector pins for green corrosion — clean with contact cleaner or replace
If sensor reads correctly, suspect the PCB socket

faultCodes.title: Condensate blockage (generic)
faultCodes.summary: The condensate discharge is blocked, causing acidic water to back up into the boiler. Extremely common in freezing weather when external condensate pipes freeze solid.
likelyCauses: Frozen external condensate pipe — the #1 winter call-out for all brands
Condensate trap blocked with debris or sludge
Condensate pipe run too long, incorrectly graded, or undersized
Internal siphon blocked
quickChecks: Check the external condensate pipe — pour warm (not boiling) water over it to thaw
Remove and clean the condensate trap inside the boiler
Check the pipe has a continuous fall — no dips where water can pool and freeze
If persistent, recommend upgrading to 32mm pipe or adding trace heating
Temporary fix in emergency: redirect condensate into a bucket and advise customer

faultCodes.title: Pump / circulation fault (generic)
faultCodes.summary: The boiler has detected inadequate water circulation. Either the pump has failed, is airlocked, or system flow is restricted.
likelyCauses: Pump seized or stuck — very common after summer when boiler hasn't run
Pump airlock — air trapped in pump housing prevents impeller spinning
System filter or strainer blocked with magnetite sludge
Zone valve or lockshield valve closed — restricting flow through the system
quickChecks: Feel the pump — it should be warm and vibrating when running
Try bleeding the pump via the bleed screw on the front (small flathead)
On some pumps, gently tap the body — a stuck impeller may free itself
Check the system filter (if fitted) — clean if full of sludge
Verify all zone valves are open and lockshields aren't fully closed
